description_en: A traditional Chinese style table lamp with circular wooden base, slender central stem, cylindrical fabric shade with bamboo frame rings, and visible bulb socket. Designed in the aesthetic of a Ming dynasty scholar's desk lamp with clean proportions and natural materials.

components.name: base
components.position_note: bottom center, resting on table surface

components.name: stem
components.description: Central vertical wooden pole, smooth or with subtle bamboo-node turning
components.position_note: centered on top of base, rising vertically through shade center

components.name: socket
components.description: Metal bulb socket and holder assembly
components.position_note: top end of stem, inside upper portion of shade

components.name: bulb
components.position_note: inserted into socket, centered within shade

components.name: shade
components.description: Cylindrical fabric lampshade, rice paper or silk texture
components.position_note: surrounding bulb and upper stem, bottom rim at approximately 0.20m height

components.name: shade_frame_top
components.description: Top bamboo or wood support ring of lampshade
components.position_note: upper rim of shade, top edge

components.name: shade_frame_bottom
components.description: Bottom bamboo or wood support ring of lampshade
components.position_note: lower rim of shade, resting on shade support attached to stem

components.name: finial
components.description: Decorative wooden knob securing shade top
components.position_note: centered on top of shade frame, screwing onto stem top

style_notes: Ming-style scholar's lamp aesthetic: base should be dark hardwood (zitan or hongmu tone) with possible subtle cloud-pattern carving on side edges (represented via texture, not geometry); stem should be slender and elegant, possibly with one or two raised bamboo-node rings; shade is natural rice paper or ivory silk with warm translucent quality; bamboo frame rings should be visible through shade material; brass or dark bronze socket hardware; avoid ornate decoration, favoring restrained literati elegance; proportions follow golden ratio with stem height roughly 3x base height
